data.content: The project uses **MSTest v3.8.0** with the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`). Tests are categorized via `[TestCategory]`. Let me run the integration tests:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run integration tests with dotnet test

data.reasoningText:
The project uses **MSTest** (v3.8.0) with the Microsoft Testing Platform. The `Directory.Build.props` has `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` set to true, which means it uses the newer Microsoft Testing Platform runner rather than VSTest.

The tests are categorized with `[TestCategory("Integration")]` and `[TestCategory("Unit")]`. The user wants to run only the integration tests.

With MSTest on the Microsoft Testing Platform, you can filter by category. The `dotnet test` command with the new testing platform uses `--filter` to filter tests.

Let me run:
```


dotnet test --filter "TestCategory=Integration"
```

This command will execute only the tests marked with the Integration category.

arguments.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-23cb50ddec87431fac530920615697e7 && dotnet test --filter \"TestCategory=Integration\" 2>&1", "description": "Run integration tests with dotnet test", "initial_wait": 60}
